old_claim.note: Coin Mint Assay Offices provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Fire-assay workshops scaled around mining valuation: assayers determined metal content in rocks to decide whether material qualified as ore worth mining.

source_shape.source_locator: The introduction says assayers determine the amount of metal in rock so people can decide whether rock should be defined as ore to be mined.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source frames fire assaying as a method for evaluating metal content in rocks before mining and ore processing decisions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That application makes mining the economic and operational context for assay workshops, while coin-mint assay offices are a different institutional lineage excluded from the corrected scope.

ontology_before: Chemical Assay Laboratories depended on coin_mint_assay_offices, preserving a coin-administration lineage inside a broad chemical-lab placeholder.
ontology_after: Metallurgical Fire Assay Workshops depend on mining as the commercial context for ore valuation, without claiming that coin-mint offices enabled Agricola-style fire assay.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chemical_assay_laboratories now depends on mining and no longer directly depends on coin_mint_assay_offices.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to government coin-mint assaying rather than ore and precious-metal fire assay workshops.
A source showed the earliest scoped workshops were administratively derived from coin-mint assay offices rather than mining valuation.
A separate early mint assay laboratory node were added and should preserve the coin_mint_assay_offices edge.
short_reason: The source-backed scope is ore valuation for mining, not coin-mint administration.
